completeness.reasoning: MISALIGNED NOTEBOOK pattern detected. The scanner will exclude all .py files because .ipynb exists, but the notebook (create_reduced_meps_dataset.ipynb) is purely data preprocessing and does NOT demonstrate the core neural weather prediction method. The core method is only in .py files that will be completely missed by the scanner.

clarity.reasoning: Most functions have docstrings, uses argparse and YAML config system, but some hardcoded paths detected in multiple files

modularity.reasoning: Well-structured with base classes and inheritance hierarchy, models should be importable independently, but training is coupled with complex datastore system

reproducibility.reasoning: Standard PyTorch ecosystem dependencies should install with some effort, all deps appear to be on PyPI, but GPU dependencies and torch-geometric version pinning may cause issues

executability.reasoning: No demo of the core neural weather prediction method exists that can run without downloading large external datasets. The existing notebook is only data preprocessing, not a demonstration of the paper's contribution.

recommendations: Create a notebook that imports and demonstrates the neural_lam models on included sample data
Include small sample weather datasets in the repository that can run without external downloads
Add a simple demo script that trains a model on synthetic or included data to showcase the core method
Remove hardcoded paths and make all paths configurable through the config system
Provide pre-trained model checkpoints for quick evaluation demos
